item.title: Dollar and VIX Are Back in Tandem as Iran War Usurps Tariff Bets
item.description: The dollar’s traditional relationship with equity market volatility has been revived by the war in Iran, a signal that haven-seeking investors are finding safety in the US assets they snubbed after last year’s tariff turmoil.

item.title: Alibaba Readies First Robot for Foray Into Crowded Chinese Arena
item.description: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. plans to soon unveil its first robot, a four-legged device that will mark its entry into an increasingly crowded but popular arena.

item.title: Iran War Lifts Africa Debt Costs That Have Surged Since Pandemic
item.description: The Iran war is raising borrowing costs for African countries that are only just recovering from the post-pandemic shock that sent debt burdens soaring.

item.title: EU to Bet on Electricification to Avert Recurring Energy Crises
item.description: The European Union will float a series of measures next week to help the continent deal with the Iran war’s energy price fallout, as well as electrify the economy to avert future fossil fuel shocks.

item.title: Amazon to Buy Satellite Operator Globalstar for $90 a Share
item.description: Amazon.com Inc. agreed to acquire satellite operator Globalstar Inc. in a roughly $11.6 billion deal that would boost the tech giant’s efforts to build its own satellite operation.

item.title: US Premarket Movers: American Airlines, Dell, Credo, Lucid
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures rise 0.2% as of 7:51 a.m. in New York on optimism that the US and Iran are considering another round of peace talks.

item.title: Citi Logs Best Returns in Five Years as Revamp Takes Hold
item.description: Citigroup Inc. traders rode a wave of volatility to push the Wall Street bank to its highest quarterly revenue in a decade, notching another success for Chief Executive Officer Jane Fraser’s turnaround plan.

item.title: Merz Says EU €90 Billion Loan for Kyiv Should Be Disbursed Now
item.description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said a European Union €90 billion ($106 billion) loan for Ukraine must be disbursed rapidly after an electoral defeat for Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban eliminated a major roadblock to the funding.

item.title: Property Mogul Targets Top Texas Finance Job With Anti-Tax Pitch
item.description: The Texas comptroller’s office, with its spreadsheets and spending reports, doesn’t typically draw ideological firebrands. But Don Huffines doesn’t plan on being a typical Texas comptroller.

item.title: Iran Weighs Pausing Hormuz Shipping to Avoid Derailing Talks
item.description: Iran is considering a short-term pause to shipments through the Strait of Hormuz to avoid testing a US blockade and scuppering a fresh round of peace talks, according to a person familiar with Tehran’s deliberations.

item.title: UK’s Reeves Calls Out Folly of US War in Iran Without Exit Plan
item.description: UK Chancellor of the Exchequer Rachel Reeves criticized US President Donald Trump over the damage his conflict in the Middle East has wreaked on the global economy and accused him of going to war without an exit strategy.

item.title: Warning Sign for Stocks Seen in Surging Inflation Expectations
item.description: Soaring inflation expectations are a warning sign for investors who have bought into the ceasefire-fueled rebound in stocks over the past few trading sessions, say some Wall Street market-watchers.

item.title: China’s Teapot Refiners Fight to Survive Deepening Oil Crisis
item.description: The country’s independent crude processors rely heavily on discounted Iranian, Russian and Venezuelan crude, putting them in a perilous position.

item.title: Macron Leads EU Push to Coordinate Social Media Bans for Minors
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron and a group of European Union leaders pushing to restrict social media use among minors are set to meet remotely on Thursday to coordinate their efforts.

item.title: France’s Lescure Sees Economy Overcoming Short-Term Slowdown
item.description: France is banking on longer-term investment, including from US firms in data centers, to help overcome a slowdown from the fallout of the conflict in the Middle East, Finance Minister Roland Lescure said.

item.title: Alibaba Just Took the Crown of Video Generation
item.description: Released under stealth last week, the Happy Horse AI video generator now ranks top of benchmarks

item.title: Lucid Names CEO as Saudi PIF, Uber Commit Another $750 Million
item.description: Lucid Group Inc. named a new chief executive officer and announced $750 million of fresh investment from its two biggest backers, moves to shore up its operations as the electric-vehicle maker approaches a crucial production stretch.

item.title: Global Trade Customers Ask Container Lines to Keep Digital Transition Moving
item.description: ‘I feel like I’m working with Fred Flintstone,’ one customs expert said

item.title: Trump Team Pitches 3% Sustained Growth Via Lower Deregulation, Rates
item.description: The White House is far more bullish on the US economy than most.

item.title: Investors Brace for Economic Growth Shock, BofA Survey Shows
item.description: Global investors grappling with the fallout from the Iran war have slashed their expectations for economic growth by the most in four years, according to the latest Bank of America Corp. survey of fund managers.

item.title: Somalia Parliament’s Mandate Expires Without Elections in Sight
item.description: Somalian lawmakers failed to elect a new president as the parliament’s current term came to an end on Tuesday, creating fresh uncertainty in the Horn of Africa nation that’s suffered years of political instability.

item.title: Czech Premier Chides Central Bank Rates for Hindering Lending
item.description: Czech Prime Minister Andrej Babis took a swipe at the central bank’s monetary policy, saying current interest rates hinder lending activity despite low inflation.

item.title: Emerging-Market Assets Rally on Iran War Resolution Bets
item.description: Emerging-market stocks moved closer to wiping out their losses since the start of the Middle East war, as speculation of further peace talks between the US and Iran lifted investor sentiment.

item.title: Italy’s Meloni Says It’s ‘Too Early’ to Tap Russian Gas
item.description: Italian Premier Giorgia Meloni said it was too early to consider tapping Russian gas despite the Iran war driving up energy prices and squeezing supply.

item.title: ASML Shares Get Harder to Value as Firm Stops Disclosing Orders
item.description: ASML Holding NV will omit the one metric that has driven its share price more than any other when it reports earnings Wednesday, injecting uncertainty into a stock nearing record highs.

item.title: Macron, Starmer to Hold Hormuz Transit Summit in Paris on Friday
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer will host a videoconference from Paris on Friday with countries willing to help restore free transit through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: UK Sovereign Sets Bond Demand Record as Yield Hunters Pile In
item.description: Bond investors are piling into debt sales from the UK sovereign to lender Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group Inc, lured by attractive yields that could soon fall back if the Middle East war comes to an end.

item.title: BE Semiconductor Hits Record High as BofA Says Market is Underestimating Demand
item.description: BE Semiconductor Industries NV shares scaled new highs on Tuesday as Bank America Corp. analysts raised their price target to a level above all others, saying strong demand for the Dutch firm’s products is underestimated.

item.title: Veolia Sees €1 Billion in Revenue From AI Industries by 2030
item.description: France’s Veolia Environnement SA plans to more than double revenue from artificial intelligence industries to €1 billion ($1.2 billion) by 2030 as it provides new services to the booming sector.

item.title: Turkey School Shooting Leaves 16 Wounded, Gunman Dead
item.description: An attacker opened fire at a high school in Turkey’s southeast, wounding 16 people before dying by suicide.
